title: Level 798
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 798: "Serene Skylights"'''}} {{Background|file=Pastel 799 main.jpg}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.cdnfonts.com/css/heather'); /* Page */ .page { border: solid 2px rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9); margin-top: 12px; position: relative; border-radius: 12px; box-shadow: 0px 0px 0px 0 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.4); } /* Darkmode CSS */ .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(90, 36, 83, 0.6); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(203, 122, 162, 0.6); --theme-link-color: rgba(235, 157, 255, 1); --theme-border-color: black; .page-side-tool { border: solid 2px color here; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px color white; } --theme-community-header-color: white; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: white; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: color here; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: white; } /* Lightmode CSS */ .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(235, 179, 255, 0.9); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(221, 124, 255, 0.9); --theme-link-color: rgba(166, 91, 185, 1); --theme-border-color: color here; .page-side-tool { border: solid 2px color here; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px color here; } --theme-community-header-color: color here; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: color here; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: color here; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: color here; }}} {{CSS|content= html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family:Jost}} {{RecommendedAudioNest|audio={{Audio|align=center|file=Dandelions forever gakhed.mp3|volume=0.7|round=yes|loop=true}}|text=enable page ambience for best experience:}} {{Headerbox|header=The air is cool, the moss is soft, everything feels... oddly perfect. It's not exactly home, but it does just fine.|file=Less jarring headerbox.jpg|speed=15s}} {{Class 0|color-h=17|color-s=100%|color-l=62%|safety=Safe|security=Secure|entity=Psychological Benefits|class=Xi|hexagon=Ξ}} {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Pastel 799 main.jpg|caption=The most well-known photo of Level 798.}} ===Appearance=== '''Level 798,''' given the title "Serene Skylights," is a complex of expansive, single-floor office lobbies dotted with skylights. The office flooring, in place of typical carpet or linoleum, is a continuous bed of moss upon a soil substrate. Rooms are spacious, though starkly empty, containing at most a drawer of minimal supplies or miscellaneous office furniture. More often than not, these drawers contain contextually nonsensical objects that may or may not be useful; examples including random wooden toys, laboratory equipment, cleaning supplies, and almost never any foodstuffs. As such, '''Level 798''' itself is suboptimal for the collection of resources. '''Level 798's''' structure, although simply summarized as a single-floor office lobby, tends to deviate heavily from the standard architecture of similar buildings on [[The Frontrooms|Earth.]] Hallways rarely turn at sharp angles - the majority of hallway stretches are instead curved. This compliments the natural design of the structure, too; rounded edges are seen on skylights and windows, more similarly to an indoor playplace. While '''Level 798's''' structure is technically comprised of one floor, this does not mean it is entirely flat either. Occasional sections of the level will ramp upwards or downwards onto planes of different height while still accessible by climbing or jumping from another area. Aside from the unusual design of the floors' connections, sliding glass doors are also common. Typical sliding glass doors will lead to the gardens of '''Level 798.''' '''Level 798's''' most common light source is the abundant skylights present in most rooms. These skylights lack any glass covering, allowing a cool breeze to flow into the building. This breeze has anomalously prevalent calming effects on wanderers and entities, relieving symptoms of mental issues attained through one's experience in the Backrooms such as anxiety, depression, and PTSD. The atmosphere present beyond the skylights is a gradient mass of warm pastel tones bearing no visible interruptions, with the most common colors being that of sunsets; light orange, lavender, and light pink are most prevalent. Since the skylights do not have any covering, one may climb onto the "roof" on the level, though this is not recommended due to the severe effects of acrophobia (the fear of heights) invading the mind as one begins to stand. Falling from the level's main structure can lead to a level-exclusive voidlike zone within the aforementioned atmosphere. {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Got damn!.jpg|caption=A simple garden hall.}} === The Gardens=== The gardens of '''Level 798''' can be accessed by entering sliding glass doors from the main area. Its appearance, like any other part of the level, contains a vibrant pastel sky with dim lighting. Skylights comprise essentially all of the ceiling, allowing more of such breeze to flow into this area, although due to the height of the garden's roof it generally produces the same effect as the main structure's breeze. The plants of Level 798's gardens grow on slightly elevated portions of the floor, giving a distinct definition between walkways and garden beds. Many plants are undocumented, save for a certain species that produces a fruit edible by humans, dubbed '''Skylight Fruit''' by the first wanderers to officially document it. {| class="fandom-table" |+Skylight Fruit Variants !Feature !Variant Properties |- |Red |A partially sweet, candy-like flavor reminiscent of apple. Decent nutritional value. |- |Orange |A hint of sour flavor with a juicy texture. Has a similar taste to watermelon, with great nutritional value. |- |White |Essentially tasteless with a chalky texture, with no known effects. Essentially no nutritional value. |- |Purple |Essentially tasteless with a juicy texture, though can provide calming and relaxing effects. This variant can be used as an syrupy oral medicine to cure ailments such as [[Hydrolitis Plague]] when crushed and mixed with [[Object 6|Moth Jelly]]. |- |Pink |A rumored variant of Skylight Fruit. Said to temporarily improve muscular and skeletal strength to an abnormal amount, lasting about 1-2 weeks. |}
The gardens of '''Level 798''' are also a region that holds notable relics of its history. Prior to the events of [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker,]] '''Level 798''' (or rather, its connections to other levels of the Backrooms) was essentially a distinct yet evident counterpart of Level 799, which had a similar appearance to [[Level 699]] or [[Level 399|399;]] that being it was mainly a complex of pools decorated with LED neon lights. As an effect of [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker,]] historians assume that the neon lights possessed an anomalous property which caused the level to break down into the cloudlike surface that it was situated on. As the lights remained, their energy, as well as the clouds and a certain "redacted" area, dispersed into the outer atmosphere of the level, causing the bright pastel sky of '''Level 798.''' The portions of the level that comprised the old architecture attempted to reform, though an office-like theme was unintentionally assembled through the "memory" of the broken matter. Some of these destroyed structures, which were unable to reform, later crashed into the gardens of '''Level 798.''' Pieces of marbly futuristic walls can still be found to this day, albeit much harder to find due to the foliage growing over these chunks. As a matter of fact, the vegetation is believed to be sustained by the pool water that once comprised the level. {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Youre telling me a ginger bred this man.jpg|caption=A manifestation in the Pastel Purgatory.}} ===The Pastel Purgatory=== The '''Pastel Purgatory''' is an area of '''Level 798''' that can be accessed by noclipping or falling from the main structure of the level. It appears as a boundless, entirely empty space that stretches in all directions perpetually; the only sight is an atmosphere of whirling pastel gradients. Much like [[The Void|The Void,]] if one were to enter this space, they would begin falling downwards, albeit with much lower terminal velocity. Falling in the Pastel Purgatory has been described to be "like a feather falling to the ground," given the grace in which one falls compared to most [[:Category:Nothingness and Non-Existence|nothingnesses.]] After falling for approximately 10-15 minutes, visual anomalies known as '''manifestations''' will begin appearing in the wanderer's vision. These '''manifestations''' share crude resemblances with memories that the individual wanderer has experienced in their childhood, taking the form of a back-alley of houses, a playground, etc. One key detail shared between all '''manifestations''' is the noticeable softened lighting and vibrant colors. '''Manifestations''' take about 30 seconds to appear, as the process of coming into sight is reminiscent of a rendering scene in a video game. Upon successfully loading, the wanderer is able to walk around and interact with this environment. It is only when curiosity fades out of the wanderer's mind in which the '''manifestation''' begins to unload. After unloading, the wanderer will awaken unharmed, lying on the soft moss of '''Level 798's''' main structure. As of now, '''manifestations''' are very poorly understood in terms of the general phenomenon. While '''manifestations''' are fully linear and physical to the wanderer experiencing them; as well as clothes and other equipment attached to one's body firmly, objects carried with them follow odd and non-Euclidean geometry. Objects are able to pass through any physical matter within said environment; this property considerably halting any potential research on the '''Pastel Purgatory.''' Additionally, while impacts are softened in '''manifestations''' for wanderers, objects anomalously experience amplified effects of speed after leaving a '''manifestation.''' For example, dropping a flashlight after it has left a '''manifestation''' will result in it having the same force upon landing as if one were to throw it. The reasoning behind these effects is entirely unknown. ===Colonies and Outposts=== Due to '''Level 798's''' general safety, as well as mental supportive properties and regular exits, it has proven to be a hotspot for groups throughout the Backrooms. ===[[M.E.G.]] Base Tzadei=== '''Base Tzadei''' is a colony set in '''Level 798''' founded by the [[M.E.G.]] It consists of approximately 20 members and will allow wanderers to join, as long as they can contribute to the colony's sustainment by bringing in resources. The most common means of bringing in resources is by a sliding glass door near the base which connects it to a certain point in [[Level 84|Level 84.]] This colony is open to trading, however, and is stained with a reputation for having somewhat unreasonable offers. ===The Vioyi Colony=== '''The Vioyi Colony''' is a colony founded by the [[Backroom Colonists|Backrooms Colonists.]] It has 34 members in '''Level 798''' as of 8/30/2024 and has a set price of 6 ounces of [[Object 1|Almond Water]] for permanent access to staying and living in the colony. '''The Vioyi Colony''' is primarily sustained by bulk trading with certain collectors and wanderers but will occasionally do offers with '''Base Tzadei''' when demand is low. ===The Skylight Explorers=== '''The Skylight Explorers''' consists of 13 members and is one of the only major independent colonies of this level. This colony is dedicated to exploring '''Level 798's''' regions in hopes of finding hotspots containing more useful supplies. This colony is majorly nomadic and will allow any member to stay for free. '''The Skylight Explorers''' sustain themselves using a well-supplied area of Level 798 containing stacks of [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and military-esque meal packets. Any members or wanderers caught "hoarding" these supplies will be threatened with [[Object 15|Firesalt,]] though fortunately there has only been one case of the Firesalt being used. Other settlements are known to reside in '''Level 798;''' though most are generally insignificant. ===Entrances and Exits=== It is important to note that this is not an exhaustive list of entrances; these simply list the most useful methods of entering '''Level 798.''' ===Entrances === *Fully submerging yourself into a pool in the [[Level 399|Neon Natatoriums of Level 399]] can rarely cause one to re-emerge from the moss. *Solving a [[Object 38|Doom Lock]] may lead to this level. *Quantum fluctuations, commonly in the outdoors of [[Level 3.14]] will lead here. *Passing through areas containing white walls in [[Level 70]] may lead here. *Certain lavender-colored buildings in [[Level 84]] can contain a sliding glass door entrance to '''Level 798.''' *Making contact with the Orb on [[Level 302]] will send one to this level. *Breaking through the 4th floor of a building in [[Level 385]] will lead to the Pastel Purgatory. *Entering a building in [[Level 387]] can lead here. *Denting a floor with a bowling ball in [[Level 499]] rarely leads to this level. *'''Thank goodness I found you in [[Level 511|that place]]. Otherwise...you would've died for sure...''' ===Exits=== *Entering rooms resembling mental hospitals behind sliding glass doors will lead to [[Level 996|Level 996.]] *Entering glass doors lead to [[level 13]]. *Entering dark, snowy forests behind sliding glass doors will lead to [[Level 76|Level 76.]] *Following rooms with exceedingly bright skylights most often leads to [[Level 399|Level 399,]] but can also lead to other hotel-themed levels. *[[The Grand Medium|lose yourself in your memories.]] *Some pathways in the garden may suddenly open up into [[Level 83]].
[[Level 799|You've been invited!]]
